problem_addressed: Building generally capable embodied agents that continuously explore, plan, and develop new skills in open-ended worlds without human intervention.
core_claim: VOYAGER is the first LLM-powered embodied lifelong learning agent that achieves open-ended exploration, skill acquisition, and novel discoveries in Minecraft without human intervention.
key_contributions: Ever-growing skill library of executable code
Iterative prompting mechanism with environment feedback, execution errors, and self-verification
novelty: Unlike prior works using hierarchical RL or LLM planners without skill libraries, VOYAGER combines bottom-up exploration, a skill library for complex behaviors, and iterative feedback loops for lifelong learning.
method_summary: VOYAGER uses GPT-4 via blackbox queries to generate executable code for actions in Minecraft, guided by an automatic curriculum, storing skills in a library, and iteratively refining programs using environment feedback and self-verification.
method_components: Skill library for storing/retrieving complex behaviors
Iterative prompting mechanism with feedback loops
key_innovation: The iterative prompting mechanism integrating environment feedback, execution errors, and self-verification to improve code generation and decide task progression.
assumptions: Access to GPT-4 API via blackbox queries
theoretical_grounding: Empirical validation through ablation studies and performance comparisons; no formal proofs provided.
baselines_compared: Not explicitly stated in provided text
stated_limitations: The abstract doesn't explicitly state limitations of the VOYAGER system.
unstated_weaknesses: The agent operates in Minecraft, which may not represent real-world complexity
Success may be constrained by Minecraft's specific game mechanics
Black-box interaction with GPT-4 may limit interpretability and controllability
Ability to handle truly novel situations is unclear
threats_to_validity: Results may not generalize beyond Minecraft
Performance might be due to overfitting to game mechanics
Evaluation metrics may not capture all aspects of agent capability
Discoveries are constrained by Minecraft's possibilities
reproducibility: medium (code is mentioned as available but full implementation details might be incomplete)
open_questions: How does VOYAGER handle truly novel situations?
How does performance scale with more complex environments?
Can this approach transfer to other domains?
gaps_this_enables: Integration of LLMs with embodied systems
relevance_to_topic: This paper is tangential to calibration-aware active learning for medical image classification as it focuses on embodied agents in Minecraft rather than medical image analysis.
limitations_stated: The abstract doesn't explicitly state limitations of the VOYAGER system.
title: Voyager: An Open-Ended Embodied Agent with Large Language Models
